demographics.living_situation: lives with a parent, who is present and helping care for them

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Parent calling on their behalf) My child's throat is burning, their neck glands are huge, and they're too weak to even lift their head off the pillow. They can barely swallow and I'm worried they can't keep any fluids down tonight.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It flared up over the last 24 hours after a stressful day yesterday and it's the worst it's been in a while — they can't drink properly and I didn't want to leave it until morning.

symptom_narrative.onset: started ramping up about 24 hours ago after a stressful day yesterday
symptom_narrative.duration: the whole thing has been going on for months, but this bad flare is about a day old
symptom_narrative.character: burning raw sore throat, big sore lumps in the neck, and a total wipe-out kind of weakness — like there's no energy in the body at all
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst crash in a while — completely bedbound, needs help to sit up or drink
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the throat, both sides of the neck, and just everywhere — no strength anywhere
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started; comes in these crashes
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying completely still in a dark quiet room; sucking on lozenges helps the throat a tiny bit
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any effort at all — even talking or sitting up makes the exhaustion worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: swollen tender neck glands and it hurts a lot to swallow
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: this has happened over and over since a really bad bout of glandular fever a few months ago — the same crash with the sore throat and swollen glands keeps coming back
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: totally bedbound tonight, can't chew food, needs a parent for everything

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-lasting exhaustion and repeated crashes ever since a bad glandular fever a few months ago
past_medical_history.when_diagnosed: a few months ago after the glandular fever
past_medical_history.how_managed: mostly rest and pacing; nothing really fixes it

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: had a stressful day yesterday which seems to have set this crash off; lives with and is being cared for by a parent tonight

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: looks very pale and exhausted, only able to speak in a faint whisper to save energy, keeping the room dark
the glands on both sides of the neck look and feel swollen and tender
strictly bedbound, needs help to sit up or drink; is still able to swallow their own saliva and is not drooling, breathing is quiet with no noisy or whistling sounds
